summary: Backend-only testing of AI Job Application System v3.0 — ran 16 pytest cases covering health, resume parsing, all 5 Claude Sonnet 4.5 agents end-to-end on a realistic QA engineer resume, applications CRUD (create/list/update-status/delete), and download endpoints (txt/pdf/docx + unsupported). All 16 tests passed in ~111s.

minor.issue: Returns 200 instead of 201 for resource creation (cosmetic REST convention)
Returns 200 with body instead of 204 No Content (cosmetic)
FastAPI on_event is deprecated; prefer lifespan context manager

critical_code_review_comments: agents.py _extract_json regex `\{.*?\}` is non-greedy and will fail to capture nested JSON fenced in ```json blocks when multiple braces exist; the fallback (first { ... last }) saves it, but the fenced branch is effectively dead/buggy. Consider greedy match or json.loads on full fenced body.

server.py uses os.environ['MONGO_URL'] / ['DB_NAME'] at import time — good (fails fast). EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is read with .get() and would silently produce LLM auth errors if missing; consider failing fast.

score_jobs attaches description/url back to ranked_jobs by id — depends on LLM preserving the input id. If LLM mutates id, attachment silently fails. Consider validating or matching by title+company fallback.
No rate-limiting / payload size limits on /api/resume/parse-file or agent endpoints; large PDFs could OOM the worker.
Applications endpoints have no pagination beyond .to_list(1000) — fine for MVP, flag for scale.
POST /api/applications returns the created doc directly but with status code 200; should ideally return 201 with Location header.
CORS allow_origins reads CORS_ORIGINS or '*' AND allow_credentials=True — combination is rejected by browsers; fine here since '*' is dev-only, but call out for prod.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates one application via /api/applications and deletes it in test_delete_application — DB is left clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend regression suite is in /app/backend/tests/test_ajas_backend.py. It exercises the entire 5-agent pipeline once with a real QA resume; total run time ~110s due to live Claude Sonnet 4.5 calls via emergentintegrations. State is shared across tests via a session-scoped `state` fixture (resume_text -> profile -> top_job -> customized_resume -> cover_letter -> app_id), so tests must run in order. If LLM is slow, increase TIMEOUT (currently 120s/call). All endpoints behave correctly; only cosmetic REST status-code suggestions remain.
